title: Genetic Algorithms for Algorithmic Trading
basicDesc: A genetic algorithm system for evolving trading strategies using Python ASTs and Bayesian optimization.
desc: Using python abstract syntax trees to represent genes, algorithms can be randomly generated to process logic between technical indicators and output trading signals.
An individual consists of a minimum of one indicator gene, two logic genes, and one signal gene to interpret logic.
Logic is arguably the most important gene and is able to compare two indicators(i2i), an indicator to the price(i2p), or compare an indicator to a constant value(i2c).
The current implementation uses the following in order: population generation, bayesian optimization(optuna), and staged pruning to find the best performing algorithm within a generated population.

title: Jupiter-Based Live Trading System
basicDesc: A live trading system using Jupiter DEX API with asynchronous market data streaming and paper trading.
desc: Through the Jupiter Decentralized Exchange API, this system takes in an algorithm(any python function) and asynchronously feeds market data to it.
Market data is streamed from sources such as binance, and trades made by the algorithm are processed through the Jupiter Aggregator.
Features a live paper trading mode for forward testing. Latency and small price mismatches(between data source and DEX) are expected.

title: Differentiable RL for Optimal Asset Allocation
basicDesc: A differentiable reinforcement learning approach for optimizing asset allocation using backpropagation.
desc: The policy uses two encoders, one creating embeddings through 1D Convolution and the other with LSTMs.
The learning/trading environment uses PyTorch's functions to allow a computational graph to be formed between the policy's trades and a metric.
In this way, the policy can be trained to directly optimize for the metric using standard backpropagation instead of traditional reinforcement learning methods.
To introduce exploration during training, the policy predicts a normal distribution of allocations(mu and log-sigma), where an allocation is randomly sampled.
Using action sampling also appears to stabilize the train loss, reducing erratic train losses that may hinder the policy.

title: BirdCLEF 2026 🐦
basicDesc: A bird sound classification model using EfficientNet-b0 with attention pooling and GLU layers.
desc: Starting from a Mel-scaled spectrogram, the efficientnet-b0 Convolutional Neural Network(with pretrained weights for faster convergence) is used as the model backbone to generate convoluted embeddings.
To efficiently reduce embedding dimensions, multi-scale adaptive average pooling combined with attention pooling give the main classifier network a widened perspective on the bird sounds.

In intermediary steps(between embedding and classification), GLUs(gated linear units) are used to create a "focusing" effect, allowing the network to learn which features to prioritize without using expensive self-attention.
It is crucial that the GLU weights are initialized close to 1, otherwise the randomly generated weights may interfere with learning.
Although performing below distilled Perch-based models, combining this architecture with time+frequency masking and temperature scaling gave a final score of 0.79241!
